author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXLVIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The subject of our meditation in this present life should be the praises of God; for the everlasting exaltation of our life hereafter will be the praise of God, and none can become fit for the life hereafter, who hath not practised himself for it now.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then now we praise God, but we pray to Him too.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Our praise is marked by joy, our prayer by groans.…On account of these two seasons, one, that which now is in the temptations and tribulations of this life, the other, that which is to be hereafter in everlasting rest and exultation; we have established also the celebration of two seasons, that before Easter and that after Easter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That which is before Easter signifieth tribulation, in which we now are; that which we are now keeping after Easter, signifieth the bliss in which we shall hereafter be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The celebration then which we keep before Easter is what we do now: by that which we keep after Easter we signify what as yet we have not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore we employ that time in fastings and prayer, this present time we spend in praises, and relax our fast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the Halleluia which we sing, which, as you know, meaneth (in Latin), Praise ye the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore that period is before the Lord’s Resurrection, this, after His Resurrection: by which time is signified the future hope which as yet we have not: for what we represent after the Lord’s Resurrection, we shall have after our own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in our Head both are figured, both are set forth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Baptism of the Lord setteth forth to us this present life of trial, for in it we must toil, be harassed, and, at last, die; but the Resurrection and Glorification of the Lord setteth forth to us the life which we are to have hereafter, when He shall come to recompense due rewards, evil to the evil, good to the good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And now indeed all the evil men sing with us, Halleluia; but, if they persevere in their wickedness, they may utter with their lips the song of our life hereafter; but the life itself, which will then be in the reality which now is typified, they cannot obtain, because they would not practise it before it came, and lay hold on what was to come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Halleluia.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise the Lord,” thou sayest to thy neighbour, he to thee: when all are exhorting each other, all are doing what they exhort others to do.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But praise with your whole selves: that is, let not your tongue and voice alone praise God, but your conscience also, your life, your deeds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now, when we are gathered together in the Church, we praise: when we go forth each to his own business, we seem to cease to praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let a man not cease to live well, and then he ever praiseth God.…It is impossible for a man’s acts to be evil, whose thoughts are good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For acts issue from thought: nor can a man do anything or move his limbs to do aught, unless the bidding of his thought precede: just as in all things which ye see done throughout the provinces, whatsoever the Emperor biddeth goeth forth from the inner part of his palace throughout the whole Roman Empire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great commotion is caused at one bidding by the Emperor as he sits in his palace!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He but moveth his lips, when he speaketh: the whole province is moved, when what he speaketh is being executed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So in each single man too, the Emperor is within, his seat is in the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he be good and biddeth good things, good things are done: if he be bad and biddeth evil things, evil things are done.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When Christ sitteth there, what can He bid, but what is good?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the devil is the occupant, what can he bid, but evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But God hath willed that it should be in thy choice for whom thou wilt prepare room, for God, or for the devil: when thou hast prepared it, he who is occupant will also rule.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, attend not only to the sound; when ye praise God, praise with your whole selves: let your voice, your life, your deeds, all sing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise ye the Lord from heaven” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As though he had found things in heaven holding their peace in the praise of the Lord, he exhorteth them to arise and praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Never have things in heaven held their peace in the praises of their Creator, never have things on earth ceased to praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is manifest that there are certain things which have breath to praise God in that disposition wherein God pleaseth them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no one praiseth aught, save what pleaseth him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And there are other things which have not breath of life and understanding to praise God, but yet, because they also are good, and duly arranged in their proper order, and form part of the beauty of the universe, which God created, though they themselves with voice and heart praise not God, yet when they are considered by those who have understanding, God is praised in them; and, as God is praised in them, they themselves too in a manner praise God.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise ye the Lord from heaven: praise Him in the high places.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First he saith, “from heaven,” then from earth; for it is God that is praised, who made heaven and earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All in heaven is calm and peaceful; there is ever joy, no death, no sickness, no vexation; there the blessed ever praise God; but we are still below: yet, when we think how God is praised there, let us have our heart there, and let us not hear to no purpose, “Lift up your hearts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us lift up our heart above, that it become not corrupted on earth: for we take pleasure in what the Angels do there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We do it now in hope: hereafter we shall in reality, when we have come thither.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise Him” then “in the high places.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise Him, all ye angels of His, praise Him, all His powers” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise Him, sun and moon; praise Him, all ye stars and light” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise Him, ye heaven of heavens, and waters that are above the heavens” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let them praise the Name of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When can he unfold all in his enumeration?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet he hath in a manner touched upon them all summarily, and included all things in heaven praising their Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as though it were said to him, “Why do they praise Him? what hath He conferred on them, that they should praise Him?” he goeth on, “for He spake, and they were made; He commanded, and they were created.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No wonder if the works praise the Worker, no wonder if the things that are made praise the Maker, no wonder if creation praise its Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this Christ also is mentioned, though we seem not to have heard His Name.…By what were they made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By the Word?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth he show in this Psalm, that all things were made by the Word?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He spake, and they were made; He commanded, and they were created.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No one speaketh, no one commandeth, save by word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath established them for the age, and for age upon age” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All things in heaven, all things above, all powers and angels, a certain city on high, good, holy, blessed; from whence because we are wanderers, we are wretched; whither because we are to return, we are blessed in hope; whither when we shall have returned, we shall be blessed indeed; “He hath given them a law which shall not pass away.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of command, think ye, have things in heaven and the holy angels received?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of command hath God given them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, but that they praise Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blessed are they whose business is to praise God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They plough not, they sow not, they grind not, they cook not; for these are works of necessity, and there is no necessity there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They steal not, they plunder not, they commit no adultery; for these are works of iniquity, and there is no iniquity there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They break not bread for the hungry, they clothe not the naked, they take not in the stranger, they visit not the sick, they set not at one the contentious, they bury not the dead; for these are works of mercy, and there there is no misery, for mercy to be shown to.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O blessed they!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think we that we too shall be like this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ah! let us sigh, let us groan in sighing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what are we, that we should be there? mortal, outcast, abject, earth and ashes!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He, who hath promised, is almighty.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him then turn himself to things on earth too, since he hath already spoken the praises of things in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise ye the Lord from the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For wherewith began he before?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise ye the Lord from heaven:” and he went through things in heaven: now hear of things on earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Dragons and all abysses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Abysses” are depths of water: all the seas, and this atmosphere of clouds, pertain to the “abyss.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where there are clouds, where there are storms, where there is rain, lightning, thunder, hail, snow, and all that God willeth should be done above the earth, by this moist and misty atmosphere, all this he hath mentioned under the name of earth, because it is very changeable and mortal; unless ye think that it raineth from above the stars.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these things happen here, close to the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes even men are on the tops of mountains, and see the clouds beneath them, and often it raineth: and all commotions which arise from the disturbance of the atmosphere, those who watch carefully see that they happen here, in this lower part of the universe.…Thou seest then what kind all these things are, changeable, troublous, fearful, corruptible: yet they have their place, they have their rank, they too in their degree fill up the beauty of the universe, and so they praise the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He turns then to them, as though He would exhort them too, or us, that by considering them we may praise the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Dragons” live about the water, come out from caverns, fly through the air; the air is set in motion by them: “dragons” are a huge kind of living creatures, greater there are not upon the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore with them he beginneth, “Dragons and all abysses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are caves of hidden waters, whence springs and streams come forth: some come forth to flow over the earth, some flow secretly beneath; and all this kind, all this damp nature of waters, together with the sea and this lower air, are called abyss, or “abysses,” where dragons live and praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think we that the dragons form choirs, and praise God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far from it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But do ye, when ye consider the dragons, regard the Maker of the dragon, the Creator of the dragon: then, when ye admire the dragons, and say, “Great is the Lord who made these,” then the dragons praise God by your voices.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fire, hail, snow, ice, wind of storms, which do His word” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore added he here, “which do His word”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many foolish men, unable to contemplate and discern creation, in its several places and rank, performing its movements at the nod and commandment of God think that God doth indeed rule all things above, but things below He despiseth, casteth aside, abandoneth, so that He neither careth for them, nor guideth, nor ruleth them; but that they are ruled by chance, how they can, as they can: and they are influenced by what they say sometimes to one another: e.g. “If it were God that gave rain, would He rain into the sea?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sort of providence,” they say, “is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Getulia is thirsty, and it rains into the sea.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They think that they handle the matter cleverly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One should say to them, “Getulia does at all events thirst, thou dost not even thirst.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For good were it for thee to say to God, “My soul hath thirsted for Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he that thus argueth is already satisfied; he thinketh himself learned, he is not willing to learn, therefore he thirsteth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if he thirsted, he would be willing to learn, and he would find that everything happeneth upon earth by God’s Providence, and he would wonder at the arrangement of even the limbs of a flea.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Attend, beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who hath arranged the limbs of a flea and a gnat, that they should have their proper order, life, motion?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider one little creature, even the very smallest, whatever thou wilt.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou considerest the order of its limbs, and the animation of life whereby it moveth; how doth it shun death, love life, seek pleasures, avoid pain, exert divers senses, vigorously use movements suitable to itself!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who gave its sting to the gnat, for it to suck blood with?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How narrow is the pipe whereby it sucketh!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who arranged all this? who made all this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art amazed at the smallest things; praise Him that is great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hold then this, my brethren, let none shake you from your faith or from sound doctrine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who made the Angel in heaven, the Same also made the worm upon earth: the Angel in heaven to dwell in heaven, the worm upon earth to abide on earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He made not the Angel to creep in the mud, nor the worm to move in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He hath assigned dwellers to their different abodes; incorruption He assigned to incorruptible abodes, corruptible things to corruptible abodes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe the whole, praise the whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then who ordered the limbs of the worm, doth He not govern the clouds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wherefore raineth He into the sea?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As though there are not in the sea things which are nourished by rain; as though He made not fishes therein, as though He made not living creatures therein.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe how the fishes run to sweet water.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wherefore, saith he, doth He give rain to the fishes, and sometimes giveth not rain to me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That thou mayest consider that thou art in a desert region, and in a pilgrimage of life; that so this present life may grow bitter to thee, that thou mayest long for the life to come: or else that thou mayest be scourged, punished, amended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how well doth He assign their properties to regions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, since we have spoken of Getulia, He raineth here nearly every year, and giveth corn every year; here the corn cannot be kept, it soon rotteth, because it is given every year; there, because it is given seldom, both much is given, and it can be kept for long.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But dost thou perchance think that God there deserteth man, or that they do not there after their own manner of rejoicing both praise and glorify God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take a Getulian from his country, and set him amid our pleasant trees; he will wish to flee away, and return to his bare Getulia.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To all places then, regions, seasons, God hath assigned and arranged what fits them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who could unfold it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet they who have eyes see many things therein: when seen, they please; pleasing, they are praised; not they really, but He who made them; thus shall all things praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was in thought of this that the spirit of the Prophet added the words, “which do His word.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not then that these things are moved by chance, which in every motion of theirs obey God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whither God willeth, thither the fire spreads, thither the cloud hurries, whether it carry in it rain, or snow, or hail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And wherefore doth the lightning sometimes strike the mountain, yet strikes not the robber?…Perhaps He yet seeketh the robber’s conversion, and therefore is the mountain which feareth not smitten, that the man who feareth may be changed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou also sometimes, when maintaining discipline, smitest the ground to terrify a child.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes too He smiteth a man, whom He will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou sayest to me, Behold, He smiteth the more innocent, and passeth over the more guilty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wonder not; death, whencesoever it come, is good to the good man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence dost thou know what punishment is reserved in secret for that more guilty man, if he be unwilling to be converted?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Would not they rather be scorched by lightning, to whom it shall be said in the end, “Depart into everlasting fire”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The needful thing is, that thou be guileless.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it an evil thing to die by shipwreck, and a good thing to die by fever?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whether he die in this way or in that, ask what sort of man he is who dieth; ask whither he will go after death, not how he is to depart from life.…Whatever then happeneth here contrary to our wish, thou wilt know that it happeneth not, save by the will of God, by His providence, by His ordering, by His nod, by His laws: and if we understand not why anything is done, let us grant to His providence that it is not done without reason: so shall we not be blasphemers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when we begin to argue concerning the works of God, “why is this?” “why is that?” and, “He ought not to have done this,” “He did this ill;” where is the praise of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast lost thy Halleluia.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Regard all things in such wise as to please God and praise the Creator.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if thou wert to happen to enter the workshop of a smith, thou wouldest not dare to find fault with his bellows, his anvils, his hammers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But take an ignorant man, who knows not for what purpose each thing is, and he findeth fault with all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if he have not the skill of the workman, and have but the reasoning power of a man, what saith he to himself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not without reason are the bellows placed here: the workman knoweth wherefore, though I know not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the shop he dareth not to find fault with the smith, yet in the universe he dareth to find fault with God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore just as “fire, hail, snow, ice, wind of storms, which do His word,” so all things in nature, which seem to foolish persons to be made at random, simply “do His word,” because they are not made save by His command.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then he mentioneth, that they may praise the Lord, “mountains and hills, fruitful trees and all cedars” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9): “beasts and all cattle, creeping things, and winged fowls” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then he goeth to men; “kings of the earth and all people, princes and all judges of the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 11): “young men and maidens, old men and young, let them praise the Name of the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ended is the praise from heaven, ended is the praise from earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For His Name only is exalted” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no man seek to exalt his own name.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou be exalted?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Subject thyself to Him who cannot be humbled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His confession is in earth and heaven” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “His confession”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it the confession wherewith He confesseth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, but that whereby all things confess Him, all things cry aloud: the beauty of all things is in a manner their voice, whereby they praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The heaven crieth out to God, “Thou madest me, not I myself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Earth crieth out, “Thou createdst me, not I myself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How do they cry out?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou regardest them, and findest this out, they cry out by thy voice, they cry out by thy regard.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Regard the heavens, it is beautiful: observe the earth, it is beautiful: both together are very beautiful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He made them, He ruleth them, by His nod they are swayed, He ordereth their seasons, He reneweth their movements, by Himself He reneweth them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these things then praise Him, whether in stillness or in motion, whether from earth below or from heaven above, whether in their old state or in their renewal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou seest all these things, and rejoicest, and art lifted up to the Maker, and gazest on “His invisible things understood by the things which are made,” “His confession is in earth and heaven:” that is, thou confesseth to Him from things on earth, thou confesseth to Him from things in heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And since He made all things, and nought is better than He, whatsoever He made is less than He, and whatsoever in these things pleaseth thee, is less than He.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not then what He hath made so please thee, as to withdraw thee from Him who made: if thou lovest what He made, love much more Him who made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the things which He hath made are beautiful, how much more beautiful is He who made them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And He shall exalt the horn of His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold what Haggai and Zachariah prophesied.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the “horn of His people” is humble in afflictions, in tribulations, in temptations, in beating of the breast; when will He “exalt the horn of His people”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When the Lord hath come, and our Sun is risen, not the sun which is seen with the eye, and “riseth upon the good and the evil,” but That whereof is said, To you that hear God, “the Sun of Righteousness shall rise, and healing in His wings;” and of whom the proud and wicked shall hereafter say, “The light of righteousness hath not shined unto us, and the sun of righteousness rose not upon us.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This shall be our summer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now during the winter weather the fruits appear not on the stock; thou observest, so to say, dead trees during the winter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who cannot see truly, thinketh the vine dead; perhaps there is one near it which is really dead; both are alike during winter; the one is alive, the other is dead, but both the life and death are hidden: summer advanceth; then the life of the one shineth brightly, the death of the other is manifested: the splendour of leaves, the abundance of fruit, cometh forth, the vine is clothed in outward appearance from what it hath in its stock.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, brethren, now we are the same as other men: just as they are born, eat, drink, are clothed, pass their life, so also do the saints.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes the very truth deceiveth men, and they say, “Lo, he hath begun to be a Christian: hath he lost his headache?” or, “because he is a Christian, what gaineth he from me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O dead vine, thou observest near thee a vine that is bare indeed in winter, yet not dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Summer will come, the Lord will come, our Splendour, that was hidden in the stock, and then “He shall exalt the horn of His people,” after the captivity wherein we live in this mortal life.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “An hymn to all His Saints.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Know ye what an hymn is?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is a song with praise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou praisest God and singest not, thou utterest no hymn: if thou singest and praisest not God, thou utterest no hymn: if thou praisest aught else, which pertaineth not to the praise of God, although thou singest and praisest, thou utterest no hymn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An hymn then containeth these three things, song, and praise, and that of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Praise then of God in song is called an hymn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then meaneth, “An hymn to all His Saints”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let His Saints receive an hymn: let His Saints utter an hymn: for this is what they are to receive in the end, an everlasting hymn.…
